title: Avocado conditioning hair mask
subtitle: A rich avocado-oil and honey treatment for dry, damaged, or colour-treated hair
excerpt: Avocado oil penetrates the hair shaft and adds weight and shine; honey is a natural humectant that draws moisture into the cuticle. Rosemary essential oil stimulates the scalp. Leave on for 20 to 30 minutes under a warm towel, then shampoo out.
sourceNotes: Avocado oil fatty acid composition and penetration into hair fibre: Rele and Mohile, Journal of Cosmetic Science (2003). Honey as a humectant and hair conditioner: Lusby et al., Journal of Medicinal Food (2002). Rosemary EO and scalp circulation: Panahi et al., Skinmed (2015).

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Mix fresh for each treatment. The honey and oil will separate on standing and the mixture does not keep well once combined.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Mix fresh and use in one application. Discard any remainder.

glossaryTerms.definition: Cold-pressed carrier oil from the avocado fruit. High in oleic acid, which gives it excellent penetrating ability into the hair shaft. Heavier than jojoba -- best for dry or thick hair types.

content.text: This mask targets dry, brittle, or colour-treated hair.

is one of the few carrier oils that penetrates the hair shaft rather than just coating it, making it especially effective for treating dryness from within. Honey retains moisture at the surface while rosemary essential oil supports scalp circulation.

items.prepNote: about 45 ml; reduce to 2 tbsp for fine hair
about 20 g; runny honey is easiest to mix
about 0.25 ml; omit during first trimester of pregnancy

content.text: If the honey is stiff, warm it for 10 seconds in the microwave to make it pourable. Mix the avocado oil, honey, and rosemary essential oil in a small bowl and stir until combined.

Apply to dry or damp hair from mid-length to the ends, avoiding the scalp unless your hair is very dry at the root. Work through to the tips. Gather the hair on top of the head and wrap in a warm, damp towel or a shower cap. Leave for 20 to 30 minutes.
Rinse out with warm water, then shampoo thoroughly -- oil masks need two rounds of shampoo to fully clear. Condition as normal. The hair will feel softer and look glossier from the first use.
Best for dry, thick, curly, or colour-treated hair. For fine or oily hair, reduce the oil to 1 tablespoon and apply only to the ends. Use once a week for damaged hair, once a month for maintenance.
